title: Artificial Intelligence Risk Management Framework (AI RMF 1.0)
summary: Voluntary framework organized around Govern, Map, Measure, and Manage functions for AI risk management.
auditUse: Use as the main organizing framework for AI risk governance and control mapping.

title: Artificial Intelligence Risk Management Framework: Generative Artificial Intelligence Profile
summary: GenAI-specific companion to the NIST AI RMF with risks and actions for generative AI systems.
auditUse: Use to adapt AI RMF control work to LLMs, multimodal models, and generative AI applications.

title: ISO/IEC 42001:2023 Artificial Intelligence Management System
summary: Specifies requirements for establishing, implementing, maintaining, and improving an AI management system.
auditUse: Use when designing auditable AI governance processes and certification-aligned controls.

title: ISO/IEC 23894:2023 Artificial Intelligence Guidance on Risk Management
summary: Provides guidance for managing risks related to AI systems.
auditUse: Use to align AI risk assessments with ISO risk management vocabulary and processes.

title: OECD AI Principles
summary: Intergovernmental principles for trustworthy AI, adopted in 2019 and updated in 2024.
auditUse: Use as a policy baseline for human-centric, rights-respecting AI governance.

title: Ethics Guidelines for Trustworthy AI
organization: European Commission High-Level Expert Group on AI
summary: Guidelines and assessment list organized around lawful, ethical, and robust AI.
auditUse: Use to structure qualitative assessment of human agency, technical robustness, privacy, transparency, fairness, and accountability.

title: AI Verify
summary: AI governance testing framework and toolkit developed by Singapore's IMDA.
auditUse: Use for measurable testing against governance principles and for examples of test report structure.

title: Artificial Intelligence Accountability Framework
summary: Framework organized around governance, data, performance, and monitoring for AI accountability.
auditUse: Use as a highly audit-friendly structure for evidence requests and federal/public-sector AI review.

title: OWASP Top 10 for Large Language Model Applications
summary: Community-maintained list of major security risks for LLM applications.
auditUse: Use for LLM application threat modeling, control testing, and security audit scoping.

title: MITRE ATLAS
summary: Knowledge base of adversary tactics and techniques against AI-enabled systems.
auditUse: Use to map AI-specific threats to controls, tests, and monitoring signals.

title: Secure AI Framework
summary: Conceptual framework for securing AI systems and adapting security controls to AI risk.
auditUse: Use to review AI security foundations, detection, response, platform controls, and feedback loops.

title: ISO/IEC 42005:2025 AI System Impact Assessment
summary: Guidance for organizations conducting AI system impact assessments.
auditUse: Use to design or evaluate AI impact assessment procedures, scope statements, stakeholder analysis, and lifecycle evidence.

title: ISO/IEC 42006:2025 Requirements for Bodies Auditing and Certifying AI Management Systems
summary: Requirements for organizations that audit and certify AI management systems against AI management standards.
auditUse: Use to understand competence, consistency, and impartiality expectations for AI management system certification audits.

title: Voluntary AI Safety Standard
organization: Australian Government Department of Industry, Science and Resources
summary: Australian voluntary standard organized around ten guardrails for safe and responsible AI use.
auditUse: Use as a practical checklist for AI governance roles, risk controls, testing, transparency, human oversight, and stakeholder engagement.

title: AI Guidelines for Business Ver. 1.0
organization: Japan Ministry of Economy, Trade and Industry and Ministry of Internal Affairs and Communications
summary: Japanese business guidelines integrating earlier AI R&D, utilization, and governance guidance for AI operators.
auditUse: Use for Japan-facing AI governance reviews across developers, providers, and deployers.

title: Model AI Governance Framework for Generative AI
organization: Singapore IMDA and AI Verify Foundation
summary: Singapore framework expanding earlier model AI governance work to generative AI through ecosystem-level governance dimensions.
auditUse: Use for GenAI governance reviews covering accountability, evaluation, security, provenance, incident reporting, and user trust.

title: ASEAN Guide on AI Governance and Ethics
summary: Regional guide for responsible AI governance and ethics across ASEAN member states.
auditUse: Use to compare organizational controls with ASEAN governance expectations and regional interoperability concerns.

title: Recommendation on the Ethics of Artificial Intelligence
summary: Global AI ethics recommendation adopted by UNESCO member states, including policy action areas and ethical impact assessment concepts.
auditUse: Use as a rights- and ethics-based benchmark for public policy, social-impact, and organizational AI governance audits.

title: Continental Artificial Intelligence Strategy
summary: African Union strategy for AI development, governance, regulation, capacity building, and inclusive digital transformation.
auditUse: Use for Africa-facing policy reviews and to understand continental priorities for AI-ready institutions and regulatory capacity.

title: Reporting Framework for the Hiroshima AI Process Code of Conduct
summary: OECD reporting framework for monitoring application of the G7 Hiroshima Process International Code of Conduct for advanced AI systems.
auditUse: Use to benchmark advanced-AI provider reporting on risk assessment, incident reporting, information sharing, and safety practices.

title: UAE Position on AI Policy and Guiding Principles
organization: UAE Minister of State for Artificial Intelligence, Digital Economy and Remote Work Applications Office
summary: UAE policy position and guiding principles for responsible and secure AI governance.
auditUse: Use for UAE-facing governance reviews and public-sector AI policy alignment.

title: AI Ethics Principles
organization: Saudi Data and Artificial Intelligence Authority
summary: Saudi national principles and controls for ethical AI development and use.
auditUse: Use for Saudi-facing AI governance reviews and for evidence mapping around fairness, human oversight, reliability, and transparency.

title: Realize the Full Potential of Artificial Intelligence
summary: Practitioner guidance connecting AI governance and risk management to COSO-style enterprise risk management.
auditUse: Use to map AI risks and controls into enterprise risk management and internal-control language.

title: Artificial Intelligence Auditing Framework
summary: Internal-audit-oriented framework for planning and performing AI audit work.
auditUse: Use to connect AI audit objectives with internal audit planning, governance, risk management, and assurance practices.

title: AI Auditing: Checklist for AI Auditing
organization: European Data Protection Board Support Pool of Experts
summary: Checklist-style AI auditing guidance commissioned through the European Data Protection Board's Support Pool of Experts.
auditUse: Use as a practical checklist for AI audit planning where privacy, data protection, and accountability requirements matter.

title: Inspect AI
summary: Open-source framework for evaluating AI systems, including LLM capabilities and safety behaviors.
auditUse: Use as a practical evaluation harness for repeatable model safety tests and audit evidence generation.

title: Preparedness Framework, Version 2
summary: OpenAI's frontier model risk framework for preparedness evaluations and risk-based deployment decisions.
auditUse: Use to compare frontier model evaluation thresholds, risk categories, mitigation gates, and deployment governance.

title: Frontier Safety Framework, Version 3.0
summary: Google DeepMind framework for identifying critical capability levels and applying mitigations for frontier AI risks.
auditUse: Use to compare frontier AI risk categories, evaluation triggers, and mitigations against other frontier developer frameworks.

title: Responsible Scaling Policy, Version 3.0
summary: Anthropic's updated responsible scaling framework for catastrophic-risk assessment and frontier AI safety governance.
auditUse: Use to inspect frontier safety roadmaps, risk reporting, capability thresholds, and safety-governance commitments.

title: AI Incident Database
summary: Public database cataloging real-world AI incidents and harms.
auditUse: Use to identify incident patterns, seed risk scenarios, and benchmark post-deployment monitoring programs.

title: AI Incidents Monitor
summary: OECD dashboard for tracking AI incidents and hazards reported across public sources.
auditUse: Use to inform incident taxonomies, risk scenario libraries, and ongoing monitoring procedures.

title: OECD Framework for the Classification of AI Systems
summary: Framework for describing and classifying AI systems by context, data/input, model, task/output, and people/planet impacts.
auditUse: Use as a structured scoping tool before selecting audit criteria, risk categories, and evidence requests.
